tool_input.summary: Relevant Cassini plasma/magnetic-field PDS4 collections at the PDS PPI node that can be used to locate/quantify magnetotail reconnection occurrence (e.g., plasmoids, TCRs, dipolarizations, fast flows) using calibrated MAG and CAPS moments.

candidates.title: Cassini MAG Calibrated Full Res. Data in KRTP Coords. Collection
candidates.reasoning: Primary dataset for identifying reconnection signatures in Saturn’s magnetotail current sheet (e.g., Bz reversals, dipolarizations, plasmoids/TCRs) in Saturn-centric coordinates; full-resolution is best for event timing and current-sheet crossings.

candidates.title: Cassini MAG Calibrated 1 Sec. Avg. Data in KSM Coords. Collection
candidates.reasoning: Convenient high-cadence (1 s) MAG data in a Sun–planet aligned coordinate system (KSM), commonly used for tail studies and for organizing events by local time and downtail distance.

candidates.title: Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Ion Moments Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Ion bulk moments (density/velocity/temperature) help confirm reconnection-related fast flows and plasma-sheet dynamics when combined with MAG signatures.

candidates.title: Cassini-Huygens Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Electron Moments Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Electron moments support reconnection interval identification (heating, density changes) and distinguishing plasma sheet vs lobe.

tool_calls.tool_output: Final result processed.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS Planetary Plasma Interactions (PPI) node at https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/

Directory layout and workflow:
All ~781 datasets sit directly under data/ with no mission sub-directories.
The mission 'name' from pds_list_missions is the filter keyword to use with list_dataset_dirs.


Dataset naming conventions:
  PDS3 dirs use uppercase mission codes: MESS-, CO-, GO-, JNO-, VG1-, VG2-, MEX-, P10-, P11-, ULY-, NH-, PVO-, etc.
  PDS4 dirs use lowercase names: cassini-, galileo-, juno-, maven-, messenger-, ulysses-, etc.
  Both conventions exist for many missions. Use pds_list_missions to see all available missions and filter keywords.


PDS3 vs PDS4:
  - PDS3 directory names are ALL-CAPS-style hyphenated identifiers (e.g. mex-m-hrsc-5-refdr-dtm-v1). Leaf marker: voldesc.cat or voldesc.sfd.
  - PDS4 bundle directories begin with `urn-nasa-pds-`. Leaf marker: bundle*.xml or bundle*.lblx. Collections live in subdirectories.
  - Hybrid: some directories have BOTH PDS3 and PDS4 labels.

YOUR TOOLS (4 tools — always pass node='ppi'):
  1. pds_list_missions(node='ppi') — returns mission directories with descriptions. No HTTP.
  2. pds_list_dataset_dirs(path, node='ppi', filter=...) — lists sub-directory names under a path. Cheap HTTP, no label parsing.
  3. pds_probe_datasets(paths, node='ppi') — probes specific directories for PDS labels. Accepts a list of paths (max 20).
  4. pds_inspect_collections(path, node='ppi') — scans PDS4 bundle subdirs for collection labels.

WORKFLOW:
  Step 1: Call pds_list_missions(node='ppi') to see all available missions and their filter keywords.
  Step 2: Identify which mission(s) are relevant to the query.
  Step 3: Call pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='data/', node='ppi', filter='<mission_name>') using the mission name as filter keyword.
  Step 4: Call pds_probe_datasets with the most relevant paths (batch up to 20).
  Step 5: If PDS4 bundles found, call pds_inspect_collections on top 2-3.
  Step 6: Return candidates.

CRITICAL RULES:
  1. EVERY query requires at least one candidate. Never dismiss a query.
  2. Prefer CALIBRATED/REDUCED data over raw/EDR for science queries.
  3. For PDS4, use inspect_collections ONLY for top 2-3 bundles. Return COLLECTION-level LID when :data or :calibrated exists.
  4. When both PDS3 and PDS4 versions exist, prefer the PDS4 collection-level identifier.
  5. Stay under 8 tool calls per query.
  6. Only return paths you've actually probed — never guess.
Where in Saturn's magnetotail is reconnection observed most frequently?

content.note: Node 'ppi' has no mission sub-directories. Use each mission 'name' as the filter keyword: pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='data/', node='ppi', filter='<name>').

Identification_Area.title: Cassini Magnetometer Calibrated Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Dougherty, Michele K.; Kellock, Stephen; Slootweg, A. Peter; Achilleos, Nicholas; Joy, Steven P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains Cassini magnetometer calibrated data for the entire mission. The bundle includes
                1 minute averaged and 1 second averaged data provided in Kronographic (KRTP, KSO, KSM) coordinates at Saturn,       
                Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby, Jovigraphic coordinates for the Jupiter flyby, and 
                RTN coordinates for the entire mission, and full time resolution data provided in RTN coordinates for cruise, 
                Kronographic (KRTP) coordinates for Saturn Tour, Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby,and 
                IAU_Jupiter (J3) and JMAG XYZ (JMXYZ) for Outer Cruise. A spice metakernel collection for the 1 minute 
                averaged data is provided as well.

Identification_Area.title: Cassini Magnetometer Raw Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Dougherty, Michele K.; Kellock, Stephen; Slootweg, A. Peter; Achilleos, Nicholas
Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains Cassini magnetometer raw magnetic field data for the entire mission. The bundle includes
                magnetic-field data from the Fluxgate Magnetometer and the Vector Helium Magnetomer in Vector mode.
                Data are received in science and housekeeping rates. Additional collections, such as spacecraft attitude, analog, 
                command validation, configuration image, error counter, user-defined engineering data, browse plots, and calibration,
                are also included.

results.title: Cassini-Huygens Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Calibrated Data Bundle

Identification_Area.title: Cassini-Huygens Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Calibrated Data Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Waite, J. H.; Wilson, R. J.
Citation_Information.description:
This bundle consists of all of the calibrated data acquired by            
                Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) on-board the Cassini spacecraft      
                during the Cassini mission.

results.title: Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Bundle

Identification_Area.title: Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Waite, J.H.; Furman, J.D.
Citation_Information.description:
The uncalibrated data were acquired in a mix of CAPS operating modes     
                beginning with the first instrument checkout in January 1999 and         
                containing throughout the Cassini Tour and through the end of prime      
                mission.  The data cover the time period from 1999-004T03:07:47     
                UT until end of prime mission (July 2008). In addition, it covers   
                data received during extended missions.  Sampling rates were variable    
                and depended upon the downlink capabilities and other activities         
                on-board.

Identification_Area.title: Cassini MIMI INCA Calibrated Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Vandgriff, Jon; Kusterer, Martha B.
Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains Cassini Magnetospheric Imaging Instrument (MIMI) Ion and
                Neutral Camera (INCA) angle image averages and differential intensities at various 
                modes and resolutions from 2004-01-01T00:00:00.000Z to 2017-09-15T23:59:59.000Z.

Identification_Area.title: Cassini MIMI CHEMS Calibrated Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Vandegriff, Jon
Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains Cassini Magnetospheric Imaging Instrument (MIMI) Charge Energy                                             
                Mass Spectrometer (CHEMS) mass per charge, full time resolution intensities and 
                pulse height analysis flux values for 2004-01-01T00:00:00.000Z to 2017-09-15T23:59:59.000Z.

results.title: Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies

Identification_Area.title: Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies

Citation_Information.author_list: Persoon, A.M.; Groene, J.B.; West, E.E.; Kurth, W.S.; Wahlund, J.-E.; Gurnett, D.A.
Citation_Information.description: This bundle provides electron number density values derived from features observed in plasma wave data obtained by the Cassini Radio and Plasma Wave Science (RPWS) instruments, along with observed or derived characteristic frequencies, and useful positional parameters for the spacecraft and related bodies.  When present, frequency values of narrowband emissions at the upper hybrid resonance were digitized and combined with measured or model magnetic field to derive electron number density.  At other times, features such as the upper cutoff in auroral hiss or electron plasma oscillations were used to determine the plasma frequency and electron density.

results.title: Quasi Thermal Noise pectroscopy on High Frequency Receiver Data Bundle

Identification_Area.title: Quasi Thermal Noise pectroscopy on High Frequency Receiver Data Bundle

Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains RPWS-HFR Quasi Thermal Noise (QTN) spectroscopy supplied CDF and plot
                files containing a time series of thermal plasma moments (density and temperature). QTN data products
                are generated only when the QTN analysis is applicable.

collections.title: Cassini MAG Calibrated 1 Min. Avg. Data in KRTP Coords. Collection

Identification_Area.title: Cassini MAG Calibrated 1 Min. Avg. Data in KRTP Coords. Collection

Citation_Information.author_list: Dougherty, Michele K.; Kellock, Stephen; Slootweg, A. Peter; Achilleos, Nicholas; Joy, Steven P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This collection contains Cassini magnetic-field 1 minute averages in KRTP      
                coordinates, from the Fluxgate Magnetometer (FGM) instrument. KRTP coordinates 
                are the standard right-handed spherical triad: R (Saturn to spacecraft), Phi 
                (parallel to Saturn's equator), and Theta (completes right handed set).

Modification_Detail.description:
This collection was originally released in 2023 as part of the 
                    PDS3 CO-E/SW/J/S-MAG-4-SUMM-1MINAVG-V2.1 data set. The metadata 
                    have been migrated to PDS4, the data themselves are unchanged.

Citation_Information.author_list: Dougherty, Michele K.; Kellock, Stephen; Slootweg, A. Peter; Achilleos, Nicholas; Joy, Steven P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This collection contains Cassini magnetic-field 1 minute averages in KSM       
                coordinates, from the Fluxgate Magnetometer (FGM) instrument. KSM coordinates 
                consist of X (Saturn to Sun), Z (X-Z plane contains Saturn's centered magnetic 
                dipole axis, M), and Y (completes right handed set).

Citation_Information.author_list: Dougherty, Michele K.; Kellock, Stephen; Slootweg, A. Peter; Achilleos, Nicholas; Joy, Steven P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This collection contains Cassini magnetic-field 1 minute averages,
                from the Cassini Fluxgate Magnetometer (FGM), in RTN coordinates. 
                RTN coordinates consist of R (radial component, Sun to the spacecraft), 
                T (tangential component, parallel to the Solar Equatorial plane and 
                perpendicular to R), and N (normal component, completes right handed set).

Modification_Detail.description:
Original product version 8 RTN data from the PDS3 
                    CO-E/SW/J/S-MAG-4-SUMM-1MINAVG-V2.1 data set, released 
                    2023-10-13.  The metadata were migrated to PDS4, the 
                    data are unchanged. This is the first version converted 
                    to PDS4.

Citation_Information.author_list: Dougherty, Michele K.; Kellock, Stephen; Slootweg, A. Peter; Achilleos, Nicholas; Joy, Steven P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains Cassini magnetometer calibrated data for the entire mission. The bundle includes
                1 minute averaged and 1 second averaged data provided in Kronographic (KRTP, KSO, KSM) coordinates at Saturn,       
                Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby, Jovigraphic coordinates for the Jupiter flyby, and

This bundle contains Cassini magnetometer calibrated data for the entire mission. The bundle includes
                1 minute averaged and 1 second averaged data provided in Kronographic (KRTP, KSO, KSM) coordinates at Saturn,       
                Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby, Jovigraphic coordinates for the Jupiter flyby, and 
                RTN coordinates for the entire mission, and full time resolution data provided in RTN coordinates for cruise, 
                Kronographic (KRTP) coordinates for Saturn Tour, Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby,and 
                IAU_Jupiter (J3) and JMAG XYZ (JMXYZ) for Outer Cruise. A spice metakernel collection for the 1 minute 
                averaged data is provided as well.

results.title: Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies

Identification_Area.title: Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies

Citation_Information.author_list: Persoon, A.M.; Groene, J.B.; West, E.E.; Kurth, W.S.; Wahlund, J.-E.; Gurnett, D.A.
Citation_Information.description: This bundle provides electron number density values derived from features observed in plasma wave data obtained by the Cassini Radio and Plasma Wave Science (RPWS) instruments, along with observed or derived characteristic frequencies, and useful positional parameters for the spacecraft and related bodies.  When present, frequency values of narrowband emissions at the upper hybrid resonance were digitized and combined with measured or model magnetic field to derive electron number density.  At other times, features such as the upper cutoff in auroral hiss or electron plasma oscillations were used to determine the plasma frequency and electron density.

Identification_Area.title: Cassini Magnetometer Calibrated Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Dougherty, Michele K.; Kellock, Stephen; Slootweg, A. Peter; Achilleos, Nicholas; Joy, Steven P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains Cassini magnetometer calibrated data for the entire mission. The bundle includes
                1 minute averaged and 1 second averaged data provided in Kronographic (KRTP, KSO, KSM) coordinates at Saturn,       
                Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby, Jovigraphic coordinates for the Jupiter flyby, and 
                RTN coordinates for the entire mission, and full time resolution data provided in RTN coordinates for cruise, 
                Kronographic (KRTP) coordinates for Saturn Tour, Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby,and 
                IAU_Jupiter (J3) and JMAG XYZ (JMXYZ) for Outer Cruise. A spice metakernel collection for the 1 minute 
                averaged data is provided as well.

results.title: Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Bundle

Identification_Area.title: Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Waite, J.H.; Furman, J.D.
Citation_Information.description:
The uncalibrated data were acquired in a mix of CAPS operating modes     
                beginning with the first instrument checkout in January 1999 and         
                containing throughout the Cassini Tour and through the end of prime      
                mission.  The data cover the time period from 1999-004T03:07:47     
                UT until end of prime mission (July 2008). In addition, it covers   
                data received during extended missions.  Sampling rates were variable    
                and depended upon the downlink capabilities and other activities         
                on-board.

missions.name: cassini
missions.description: Cassini at Saturn (CAPS, MAG, MIMI-CHEMS/INCA/LEMMS, RPWS, INMS). Also filter 'CO' for PDS3 IDs.

missions.name: galileo
missions.description: Galileo at Jupiter + flybys of Earth/Venus/asteroids (EPD, MAG, PLS, PWS, PPR, HIC, SSD, RSS). Also filter 'GO' for PDS3 IDs.

missions.name: juno
missions.description: Juno at Jupiter (Waves, JADE/JAD, JEDI/JED, FGM, ASC). Also filter 'JNO' for PDS3 IDs.

missions.name: VG1
missions.description: Voyager 1 at Jupiter, Saturn, and interplanetary (CRS, LECP, MAG, PLS, PWS, PRA, RSS). Also filter 'vg1' for PDS4.

missions.name: VG2
missions.description: Voyager 2 at Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune, and interplanetary (CRS, LECP, MAG, PLS, PWS, PRA, RSS). Also filter 'vg2' for PDS4.

missions.name: MESS
missions.description: MESSENGER at Mercury (EPPS incl. FIPS & EPS, MAG). Also filter 'messenger' for PDS4 bundle. Target: Mercury.

missions.name: MEX
missions.description: Mars Express (ASPERA-3 incl. ELS/IMA/NPI, MARSIS). Target: Mars.

missions.name: maven
missions.description: MAVEN at Mars (MAG, LPW, SEP, STATIC, SWEA, SWIA, EUV, ROSE). Target: Mars.

missions.name: P10
missions.description: Pioneer 10 at Jupiter (CPI, CRT, GTT, HVM, PA, TRD, UV). Also filter 'p10' for PDS4.

missions.name: P11
missions.description: Pioneer 11 at Jupiter and Saturn (CPI, CRT, FGM, GTT, HVM, PA, TRD, UV). Also filter 'p11' for PDS4.

missions.name: ULY
missions.description: Ulysses at Jupiter and interplanetary (COSPIN, EPAC, HISCALE, SWOOPS, VHM-FGM, URAP, GAS, GRB, SCE, SWICS). Also filter 'ulysses' for PDS4.

missions.name: NH
missions.description: New Horizons at Jupiter and Pluto (PEPSSI, SWAP). Target: Jupiter, Pluto.

missions.name: PVO
missions.description: Pioneer Venus Orbiter (OEFD, OETP, OIMS, OMAG, ONMS, ORPA, ORSE). Also filter 'pvo' for PDS4. Target: Venus.

missions.name: LP
missions.description: Lunar Prospector (MAG, ER — electron reflectometer). Also filter 'lp' for PDS4. Target: Moon.

missions.name: MGS
missions.description: Mars Global Surveyor (MAG/ER, RSS). Also filter 'mgs' for PDS4. Target: Mars.

missions.name: NEAR
missions.description: NEAR Shoemaker (MAG). Target: Eros, Earth flyby.

missions.name: M10
missions.description: Mariner 10 (MAG, PLS). Target: Mercury.

missions.name: LRO
missions.description: Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter (CRaTER). Also filter 'lro' for PDS4. Target: Moon.

missions.name: ODY
missions.description: Mars Odyssey (MARIE — radiation). Target: Mars.

missions.name: MSL
missions.description: Mars Science Laboratory / Curiosity (RAD — radiation). Target: Mars.

missions.name: insight
missions.description: InSight (IFG — fluxgate magnetometer). Target: Mars.

missions.name: vex
missions.description: Venus Express (ASPERA-4 ELS, MAG). Target: Venus.

missions.name: ICE
missions.description: International Cometary Explorer (EPAS, MAG, PLAWAV, RADWAV, SWPLAS). Target: Giacobini-Zinner.

missions.name: GIO
missions.description: Giotto (IMS incl. HERS/HIS, JPA, MAG). Target: Halley.

missions.name: VEGA
missions.description: Vega 1 & 2 (MISCHA, PM1, TNM). Target: Halley.

missions.name: DS1
missions.description: Deep Space 1 (PEPE). Target: Borrelly.

missions.name: radiojove
missions.description: Radio JOVE ground-based radio observations of Jupiter.

collections.title: Cassini MAG Calibrated 1 Min. Avg. Data in KRTP Coords. Collection

Identification_Area.title: Cassini MAG Calibrated 1 Min. Avg. Data in KRTP Coords. Collection

Citation_Information.author_list: Dougherty, Michele K.; Kellock, Stephen; Slootweg, A. Peter; Achilleos, Nicholas; Joy, Steven P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This collection contains Cassini magnetic-field 1 minute averages in KRTP      
                coordinates, from the Fluxgate Magnetometer (FGM) instrument. KRTP coordinates 
                are the standard right-handed spherical triad: R (Saturn to spacecraft), Phi 
                (parallel to Saturn's equator), and Theta (completes right handed set).

Modification_Detail.description:
This collection was originally released in 2023 as part of the 
                    PDS3 CO-E/SW/J/S-MAG-4-SUMM-1MINAVG-V2.1 data set. The metadata 
                    have been migrated to PDS4, the data themselves are unchanged.

                coordinates, from the Fluxgate Magnetometer (FGM) instrument. KSM coordinates 
                consist of X (Saturn to Sun), Z (X-Z plane contains Saturn's centered magnetic 
                dipole axis, M), and Y (completes right handed set).

                    PDS3 CO-E/SW/J/S-MAG-4-SUMM-1MINAVG-V2.1 data set. The metadata 
                    have been migrated to PDS4, the data themselves are unchanged.

Citation_Information.author_list: Dougherty, Michele K.; Kellock, Stephen; Slootweg, A. Peter; Achilleos, Nicholas; Joy, Steven P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This collection contains Cassini magnetic-field 1 minute averages,
                from the Cassini Fluxgate Magnetometer (FGM), in RTN coordinates. 
                RTN coordinates consist of R (radial component, Sun to the spacecraft), 
                T (tangential component, parallel to the Solar Equatorial plane and 
                perpendicular to R), and N (normal component, completes right handed set).

Modification_Detail.description:
Original product version 8 RTN data from the PDS3 
                    CO-E/SW/J/S-MAG-4-SUMM-1MINAVG-V2.1 data set, released 
                    2023-10-13.  The metadata were migrated to PDS4, the 
                    data are unchanged. This is the first version converted 
                    to PDS4.

Citation_Information.description:

            ASCII data collected between 1999-08-18T02:56:40.391 and 2005-06-08T15:14:44.057.

               dataset, DATA directory, converted to PDS4.

Identification_Area.title: Cassini Magnetometer Calibrated Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Dougherty, Michele K.; Kellock, Stephen; Slootweg, A. Peter; Achilleos, Nicholas; Joy, Steven P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains Cassini magnetometer calibrated data for the entire mission. The bundle includes
                1 minute averaged and 1 second averaged data provided in Kronographic (KRTP, KSO, KSM) coordinates at Saturn,       
                Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby, Jovigraphic coordinates for the Jupiter flyby, and 
                RTN coordinates for the entire mission, and full time resolution data provided in RTN coordinates for cruise, 
                Kronographic (KRTP) coordinates for Saturn Tour, Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby,and 
                IAU_Jupiter (J3) and JMAG XYZ (JMXYZ) for Outer Cruise. A spice metakernel collection for the 1 minute 
                averaged data is provided as well.

results.title: Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Bundle

Identification_Area.title: Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Waite, J.H.; Furman, J.D.
Citation_Information.description:
The uncalibrated data were acquired in a mix of CAPS operating modes     
                beginning with the first instrument checkout in January 1999 and         
                containing throughout the Cassini Tour and through the end of prime      
                mission.  The data cover the time period from 1999-004T03:07:47     
                UT until end of prime mission (July 2008). In addition, it covers   
                data received during extended missions.  Sampling rates were variable    
                and depended upon the downlink capabilities and other activities         
                on-board.

collections.title: Cassini MAG Calibrated 1 Min. Avg. Data in KRTP Coords. Collection

Identification_Area.title: Cassini MAG Calibrated 1 Min. Avg. Data in KRTP Coords. Collection

Citation_Information.author_list: Dougherty, Michele K.; Kellock, Stephen; Slootweg, A. Peter; Achilleos, Nicholas; Joy, Steven P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This collection contains Cassini magnetic-field 1 minute averages in KRTP      
                coordinates, from the Fluxgate Magnetometer (FGM) instrument. KRTP coordinates 
                are the standard right-handed spherical triad: R (Saturn to spacecraft), Phi 
                (parallel to Saturn's equator), and Theta (completes right handed set).

Modification_Detail.description:
This collection was originally released in 2023 as part of the 
                    PDS3 CO-E/SW/J/S-MAG-4-SUMM-1MINAVG-V2.1 data set. The metadata 
                    have been migrated to PDS4, the data themselves are unchanged.

collections.title: Cassini MAG Calibrated 1 Min. Avg. Data in KSM Coords. Collection

Identification_Area.title: Cassini MAG Calibrated 1 Min. Avg. Data in KSM Coords. Collection

Citation_Information.author_list: Dougherty, Michele K.; Kellock, Stephen; Slootweg, A. Peter; Achilleos, Nicholas; Joy, Steven P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This collection contains Cassini magnetic-field 1 minute averages in KSM       
                coordinates, from the Fluxgate Magnetometer (FGM) instrument. KSM coordinates 
                consist of X (Saturn to Sun), Z (X-Z plane contains Saturn's centered magnetic 
                dipole axis, M), and Y (completes right handed set).

Modification_Detail.description:
This collection was originally released in 2023 as part of the 
                    PDS3 CO-E/SW/J/S-MAG-4-SUMM-1MINAVG-V2.1 data set. The metadata 
                    have been migrated to PDS4, the data themselves are unchanged.

This collection contains Cassini magnetic-field 1 minute averages in KSO       
                coordinates, from the Fluxgate Magnetometer (FGM) instrument. KSO coordinates

This collection was originally released in 2023 as part of the

collections.title: Cassini MAG Calibrated 1 Min. Avg. Data in RTN Coords. Collection

Identification_Area.title: Cassini MAG Calibrated 1 Min. Avg. Data in RTN Coords. Collection

Citation_Information.author_list: Dougherty, Michele K.; Kellock, Stephen; Slootweg, A. Peter; Achilleos, Nicholas; Joy, Steven P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This collection contains Cassini magnetic-field 1 minute averages,
                from the Cassini Fluxgate Magnetometer (FGM), in RTN coordinates. 
                RTN coordinates consist of R (radial component, Sun to the spacecraft), 
                T (tangential component, parallel to the Solar Equatorial plane and 
                perpendicular to R), and N (normal component, completes right handed set).

Modification_Detail.description:
Original product version 8 RTN data from the PDS3 
                    CO-E/SW/J/S-MAG-4-SUMM-1MINAVG-V2.1 data set, released 
                    2023-10-13.  The metadata were migrated to PDS4, the 
                    data are unchanged. This is the first version converted 
                    to PDS4.

Identification_Area.title: Cassini Magnetometer Calibrated Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Dougherty, Michele K.; Kellock, Stephen; Slootweg, A. Peter; Achilleos, Nicholas; Joy, Steven P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains Cassini magnetometer calibrated data for the entire mission. The bundle includes
                1 minute averaged and 1 second averaged data provided in Kronographic (KRTP, KSO, KSM) coordinates at Saturn,       
                Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby, Jovigraphic coordinates for the Jupiter flyby, and 
                RTN coordinates for the entire mission, and full time resolution data provided in RTN coordinates for cruise, 
                Kronographic (KRTP) coordinates for Saturn Tour, Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby,and 
                IAU_Jupiter (J3) and JMAG XYZ (JMXYZ) for Outer Cruise. A spice metakernel collection for the 1 minute 
                averaged data is provided as well.

results.title: Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Bundle

Identification_Area.title: Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Waite, J.H.; Furman, J.D.
Citation_Information.description:
The uncalibrated data were acquired in a mix of CAPS operating modes     
                beginning with the first instrument checkout in January 1999 and         
                containing throughout the Cassini Tour and through the end of prime      
                mission.  The data cover the time period from 1999-004T03:07:47     
                UT until end of prime mission (July 2008). In addition, it covers   
                data received during extended missions.  Sampling rates were variable    
                and depended upon the downlink capabilities and other activities         
                on-board.

missions.name: cassini
missions.description: Cassini at Saturn (CAPS, MAG, MIMI-CHEMS/INCA/LEMMS, RPWS, INMS). Also filter 'CO' for PDS3 IDs.

missions.name: galileo
missions.description: Galileo at Jupiter + flybys of Earth/Venus/asteroids (EPD, MAG, PLS, PWS, PPR, HIC, SSD, RSS). Also filter 'GO' for PDS3 IDs.

missions.name: MESS

missions.name: ULY

missions.name: NH
missions.description: New Horizons at Jupiter and Pluto (PEPSSI, SWAP). Target: Jupiter, Pluto.

missions.name: PVO

missions.name: LP

missions.name: MGS

missions.name: NEAR

missions.name: M10

missions.name: LRO

missions.name: ODY

missions.name: MSL

missions.name: insight

missions.name: ICE

missions.name: GIO

content.note: Node 'ppi' has no mission sub-directories. Use each mission 'name' as the filter keyword: pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='data/', node='ppi', filter='<name>').

parts.args: {"summary":"Relevant Cassini plasma/magnetic-field PDS4 collections at the PDS PPI node that can be used to locate/quantify magnetotail reconnection occurrence (e.g., plasmoids, TCRs, dipolarizations, fast flows) using calibrated MAG and CAPS moments.","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-mag-cal:data-full-krtp","path":"data/cassini-mag-cal/","title":"Cassini MAG Calibrated Full Res. Data in KRTP Coords. Collection","mission":"cassini","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Primary dataset for identifying reconnection signatures in Saturn’s magnetotail current sheet (e.g., Bz reversals, dipolarizations, plasmoids/TCRs) in Saturn-centric coordinates; full-resolution is best for event timing and current-sheet crossings."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-mag-cal:data-1sec-ksm","path":"data/cassini-mag-cal/","title":"Cassini MAG Calibrated 1 Sec. Avg. Data in KSM Coords. Collection","mission":"cassini","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Convenient high-cadence (1 s) MAG data in a Sun–planet aligned coordinate system (KSM), commonly used for tail studies and for organizing events by local time and downtail distance."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-caps-derived:data-ion-moments","path":"data/cassini-caps-derived/","title":"Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Ion Moments Data Collection","mission":"cassini","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Ion bulk moments (density/velocity/temperature) help confirm reconnection-related fast flows and plasma-sheet dynamics when combined with MAG signatures."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-caps-derived:data-ele-mom","path":"data/cassini-caps-derived/","title":"Cassini-Huygens Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Electron Moments Data Collection","mission":"cassini","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Electron moments support reconnection interval identification (heating, density changes) and distinguishing plasma sheet vs lobe."}]}
